modules.name: Claim Intake
modules.scope_in:
- Digital claim submission via chatbot conversation
- Capture of claim type, incident details, and supporting documents
- Initial structural validation of submitted data
- Hand-off of validated claim payload to triage
modules.scope_out:
- Identity verification (owned by MOD-002 Customer Identity)
- Document intelligent validation (owned by MOD-003 Document Processing)
- Claim adjudication (owned by Claims Operations, out of v1 scope)
modules.summary: Captures new insurance claims initiated by authenticated customers through the chatbot, validates the structural completeness of the submission, and hands off the claim payload to downstream triage.

actors.notes: Initiates claim submission through chatbot conversation
Validates that customer is authenticated before intake
Receives uploaded documents for OCR + validation

responsibilities.responsibility: Accept claim submission from chatbot UI
Validate structural completeness of claim fields
Emit claim_submitted event when intake completes

interfaces.name: Claim Submission Conversation
interfaces.purpose: Step-by-step chatbot dialog to capture incident details

interfaces.name: verifyIdentity
interfaces.purpose: Confirm authenticated session before accepting intake

data_entities.business_purpose: Stores in-progress claim during intake conversation

frs_backlog.title: Digital Claim Submission Flow
frs_backlog.description: Step-by-step chatbot dialog that captures claim type, incident details, and submitter context; validates structural completeness before persisting the draft.

frs_backlog.title: Supporting Document Upload
frs_backlog.description: Customer attaches supporting documents to a claim draft; triggers asynchronous OCR + intelligent validation via Document Processing.

frs_backlog.title: Claim Hand-off to Triage
frs_backlog.description: On successful structural validation, emit claim_submitted event with the validated payload for downstream triage.

modules.name: Customer Identity
modules.scope_in:
- Authentication of customers using policy number + DOB or SSO
- Session management for the chatbot conversation
- Authorization checks for claim actions
modules.scope_out:
- Operator authentication (out of v1 scope)
- Identity-document verification (owned by Document Processing for OCR-based ID checks)
modules.summary: Verifies customer identity at the start of every chatbot conversation and manages the session lifecycle so downstream modules can trust the caller.

responsibilities.responsibility: Validate policy number + customer DOB or SSO assertion
Issue, refresh, and expire chatbot session tokens
Provide authorization context to downstream modules

interfaces.name: verifyIdentity
interfaces.purpose: Identity check before claim intake begins

interfaces.name: Authentication Dialog
interfaces.purpose: Collect identity credentials at start of conversation

frs_backlog.title: Customer Identity Verification
frs_backlog.description: Verify customer identity using policy + DOB or SSO; issue session token on success.

frs_backlog.title: Chatbot Session Lifecycle
frs_backlog.description: Issue, refresh, and expire session tokens; revoke on logout or inactivity.

modules.name: Document Processing
modules.scope_in:
- Asynchronous OCR and structured extraction from uploaded documents
- Intelligent validation against expected document templates
- Storage of processed documents and extraction results
modules.scope_out:
- Document upload UI (owned by Claim Intake)
- Long-term archival (handled by enterprise storage policy)
modules.summary: Processes documents attached to a claim asynchronously: OCR, structured extraction, validation, and persistence of results so triage can consume them.

responsibilities.responsibility: Trigger OCR for each uploaded document
Validate extracted fields against expected templates
Persist extraction results for downstream consumers

interfaces.name: documents_processed
interfaces.purpose: Notify triage that documents are ready

data_entities.business_purpose: Stores OCR results + validation outcome per document

frs_backlog.title: Document OCR Pipeline
frs_backlog.description: Receive documents_uploaded event, call OCR provider, persist extraction results.

frs_backlog.title: Extraction Validation
frs_backlog.description: Validate extracted fields against expected templates; flag inconsistencies for triage.

modules.name: Conversational Assistance
modules.scope_in:
- Natural language understanding of customer queries
- Self-service information guidance for FAQs and policy questions
- Escalation to human agent when bot confidence is low
modules.scope_out:
- Underlying NLU model training (handled by Platform/AI Engineering)
- Operator desktop (out of v1 scope)
modules.summary: Handles free-form customer queries during the conversation, provides self-service answers from policy + claims knowledge bases, and escalates to a human agent when confidence drops below threshold.

actors.notes: Source of FAQ and policy content

responsibilities.responsibility: Interpret customer intent from natural language input
Provide self-service answers from knowledge base
Escalate to human agent on low confidence or explicit request

interfaces.name: searchKnowledge
interfaces.purpose: Look up FAQs and policy content

interfaces.name: escalation_requested
interfaces.purpose: Hand off conversation to human agent

data_entities.business_purpose: Stores each customer utterance and bot response with confidence score

frs_backlog.title: Natural Language Understanding
frs_backlog.description: Interpret customer utterances, classify intent, and extract relevant entities.

frs_backlog.title: Self-Service Knowledge Lookup
frs_backlog.description: Query knowledge base for FAQ / policy content matching the customer's intent.

frs_backlog.title: Human Agent Escalation
frs_backlog.description: Detect low-confidence or explicit-request scenarios; hand off the live conversation to a human agent with full context.

modules.name: Claims Notifications
modules.scope_in:
- Real-time claim status updates pushed to customers
- Proactive notifications when status changes or actions are needed
modules.scope_out:
- Marketing communications (out of scope)
- Notification template authoring (handled by content team)
modules.summary: Delivers timely status updates and proactive notifications to customers about their claims, via in-chatbot messages and external channels.

responsibilities.responsibility: Listen to claim status events and decide notification eligibility
Deliver via the customer's preferred channel

data_entities.business_purpose: Audit trail of every notification sent, including channel and delivery status

frs_backlog.title: Status Change Notification
frs_backlog.description: Subscribe to claim status events and dispatch notifications when status materially changes.

frs_backlog.title: Multi-channel Delivery
frs_backlog.description: Deliver notifications via the customer's preferred channel (in-chat, email, SMS, push).

modules.name: Cross-cutting Standards
modules.scope_in:
- Common error response envelope used by every backend service
- Audit logging convention applied to all material actions
- RBAC base policies referenced by every feature module
- Shared reference data (claim types, severity tiers, status codes)
modules.scope_out:
- Module-specific business rules (handled per module)
- Infrastructure setup (architecture concern)
modules.summary: Foundation FRSes shared by every feature module: error envelope, audit logging, RBAC base, and reference data. Feature FRS reference these via depends_on rather than duplicating rules.

data_entities.business_purpose: Standardized audit trail row format used by all feature modules

frs_backlog.title: Common Error Response Envelope
frs_backlog.description: Standardize error response shape across all backend services (status code, business code, message, correlation id, hints).

frs_backlog.title: Audit Logging Standard
frs_backlog.description: Define the audit event schema and retention policy referenced by all feature modules.

frs_backlog.title: RBAC Base Policies
frs_backlog.description: Base RBAC roles + permissions referenced by every feature module's security spec.

spec_decisions.question: Should chatbot session tokens be stored server-side (Redis) or client-side (signed JWT)?

options.label: Server-side (Redis)
options.description: Opaque session ID held by client; full session data in Redis with TTL.
options.implications: Easier revocation; adds Redis dependency; supports immediate session invalidation.

options.label: Client-side (signed JWT)
options.description: Stateless JWT signed with rotating key; client holds full payload.
options.implications: No Redis dependency; harder to revoke mid-session; requires careful key rotation.

spec_decisions.recommended_rationale: Consistency with existing platform — Redis is already in use; easier revocation supports the audit requirements in MOD-000.

spec_decisions.question: Should document OCR run synchronously (block intake) or asynchronously (event-driven)?

options.label: Asynchronous (event-driven)
options.description: Intake emits documents_uploaded event; Document Processing consumes asynchronously and emits documents_processed when complete.
options.implications: Lower latency for intake; requires event bus; customer sees progressive status updates.

options.label: Synchronous (blocking)
options.description: Intake calls OCR pipeline directly and waits for result before completing.
options.implications: Simpler flow; intake latency can be 10-30s depending on document complexity; risk of timeout.

spec_decisions.recommended_rationale: Asynchronous keeps the intake conversation responsive; aligns with the rest of the architecture's event-driven hand-offs (claim_submitted, status_changed).
